paragraphText: To a solution of 4-bromobenzaldehyde (1.85 g, 10.0 mmol) in THF (20 mL) was added phenylboronic acid (1.34 g, 11.0 mmol, 1.1 equiv) and Pd(PPh3)4 (0.58 g, 0.50 mmol, 5 mol%). The mixture was heated to 80 °C for 12 h under nitrogen. After cooling to room temperature, the reaction was quenched with saturated NH4Cl (20 mL) and extracted with EtOAc (3 × 30 mL). The combined organic layers were dried over Na2SO4, filtered, and concentrated under reduced pressure to give 4-phenylbenzaldehyde (1.64 g, 90%) as a white solid.

expectedFindings.notes: N2 atmosphere during reaction, aqueous workup after quench — valid transition.
Pd catalyst present and quench step detected.

reviewerNotes: Clean Suzuki coupling with correct stoichiometry and complete workup.

paragraphText: NaBH4 (0.50 g, 13.2 mmol) was added portionwise to a solution of the ketone (2.00 g, 10.0 mmol) in MeOH (30 mL) at 0 °C. The reaction was stirred for 2 h, then poured into water (50 mL).

expectedFindings.notes: NaBH4: 0.50g / 13.2mmol = 37.9 g/mol — matches known MW of 37.83.
NaBH4 in MeOH is reactive — generates H2. Commonly used but technically incompatible.

reviewerNotes: NaBH4/MeOH is a textbook reduction. The incompatibility flag is technically correct but may be considered a false alarm in practice.

paragraphText: The aldehyde (500 mg, 3.0 mmol) was dissolved in DCM. TEA was added. The product was obtained in 82% yield. Separately, the same aldehyde gave 15% yield under different conditions.

expectedFindings.notes: 82% vs 15% yield — genuine contradiction (same entity, same unit).
DCM is explicitly stated as solvent.
No temperature specified for either reaction.

reviewerNotes: Contradictory yields plus missing temperature — should flag both.
